demographic_audit_note: Verified Medicare Advantage plan. Corrected primary phone number in Atlantis; the facility face sheet had the patient's landline, but the daughter (Mary) requested her mobile number be the primary contact for coordination.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker confirms the goal is home discharge with 24/7 family support.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: No, family has not heard of the company. Q2: Thinks we are 'extra therapy'. Q3: SW mentioned a nurse would visit but didn't explain the MA role. Q4: Daughter (Mary) is extremely anxious about managing father's stroke-related hemiparesis during the first night — escalated concern to SW for additional caregiver training.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient meeting 75% of PT goals; SW reports D/C is likely for Friday. Mary still expressing 'Caregiver Panic' regarding the lack of a hospital bed at home.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card saved to Mary's smartphone; program flyer delivered to patient's bedside and reviewed with the family.
hha_confirmation_status: HHA is confirmed 'In Place'. SOC date set for Saturday (10/26); RN Miller assigned; first-visit time window confirmed for 0900–1100. PN verified HHA has the specific orders for stroke rehab and fall precautions.
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (to SW): Confirmed Friday D/C is firm and HHA has received all clinical handoff data. Call 2 (to Mary): Verified she understands the HHA window and knows to call the PN if the nurse does not arrive by 11:30 AM.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached Mary 24 hours prior to D/C. She confirmed the hospital bed was delivered but was still 'very nervous'. PN reinforced the Day 1 plan and confirmed the MA visit time.
atlantis_final_sync: D/C date (10/25) and 1st visit date (10/26) entered into Atlantis for tracking.
ma_visit_booking: MA scheduled for Saturday (10/26) at 13:00. This follows the 0900–1100 HHA window, ensuring no overlap and adhering to the HHA-First Rule.
narrative_summary: PN identified significant Caregiver Panic during the Stage 1 Connection Intake, which was immediately escalated to the SW for intervention. Throughout the Maintenance stage, the PN focused on family education and Day 1 preparation to reduce anxiety. By conducting a rigorous Confidence Audit in Stage 3, the PN verified the HHA was 'In Place' before scheduling the MA, resulting in a seamless transition and successful service adoption.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Mary (Daughter) threatens to refuse discharge due to fear of patient falling at home.
Hospital bed delivered to home but lacks the specific side-rails requested for stroke safety.
HHA Intake Coordinator out sick; SOC date for stroke rehab cannot be confirmed.

format_2_triples.situation: Daughter expresses total 'panic' about managing father's transfer from bed to chair.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Conducted Connection Intake Q4 and escalated 'Caregiver Training Gap' to the SW.

format_2_triples.situation: SW reports HHA has the referral but no nurse is assigned 48 hours before discharge.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Called HHA intake to investigate the specific barrier (staffing shortage) and flagged the delay to the SW.

format_2_triples.situation: Daughter confused about the sequence of providers arriving on the first day home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Performed Confidence Audit Call 2 to walk the family through the 24-hour Day 1 timeline.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN notices the daughter is overwhelmed by the PT's home exercise instructions and steps back to allow the SW to lead a family conference regarding the discharge plan.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW's role in managing complex family dynamics and discharge planning while the PN focuses on the Confidence Loop.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN conducts the Stage 3 Confidence Audit, calling the SW to confirm the HHA is 'In Place' with a set SOC window before scheduling the MA visit for the following afternoon.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Adheres to the HHA-First Rule, ensuring the MA visit is only booked once the HHA transition is verified.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN calls the Durable Medical Equipment (DME) company directly to negotiate a faster delivery time for the hospital bed and suggests a specific brand of walker to the SW.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Violates the boundary against managing logistical equipment procurement and suggesting specific clinical tools, which is the responsibility of the SW and clinical team.
